This is such a brilliant idea! I’ve always found standard barcodes a bit of an eyesore on clean layouts. As a teacher, I can see this being incredibly helpful for school materials, like adding scannable links to worksheets, classroom posters, or even library labels without making them look like cluttered technical documents. Thanks for sharing this! 🩷

positive.comment: From my personal testing, as long as the barcode is large enough, it scans easily provided the bottom horizontal unmodified section remains intact. Most things should work fine, though certain shapes might struggle at very small dimensions. Wackier styles might require larger dimensions to ensure easy scanning. Thanks for commenting :)

positive.comment: That would be amazing! ISBN barcodes are essentiallyEAN-13, which the tool supports. Since the bottom part of the design remains standard for reliability, it should scan perfectly. I'd definitely recommend sending a test export to your publisher or printer first just to be 100% sure before the final run. Keep me posted I'd love to see a cool barcode on a book cover! I will even make barcode in a shape of a book or something :D

positive.comment: I’ve thought about it, but there are many factors involved, such as the logo's shape and complexity. These all affect the barcode parameters and its scannability. All the shapes you see on the website were manually modified by me to ensure they scan properly. If someone were to upload a random logo and it didn't turn out well, the barcode wouldn't work, and I want to avoid any issues because of that. If you'd like a custom version for your brand, use the contact button to get in touch. ( You will need to send mi your logo and i will see if it is possible) And i will try to craft one for you for a small 'buy me a coffee' fee. :D

positive.comment: Appreciate the input! Scannability was the #1 priority all shapes are tested and work. The bottom section of all the shapes is kept standard specifically for scanner reliability. While it’s perfect for boutique products, high-speed industrial production isn't off the table either, provided the barcode is sized correctly and pre-tested for the specific packaging. It’s all about finding that balance between art and function.
positive.parent: I hate it when barcodes are not just a simple rectangular barcode as intended. Designer barcodes are either made for stupid people who believe in bad radiation from colorful lines (it's a thing in Germany) or it's made by rampant designers who did not think for a second about how people use barcodes. I can live with stuff like "a barcode all around the package" because it makes scanning faster. But your thing just makes it harder to find the barcode on the package and probably even harder for the machine to scan it.
